chapter_title.ar: باب صِفَةِ الْغُسْلِ وَ الْوُضُوءِ قَبْلَهُ وَ بَعْدَهُ وَ الرَّجُلِ يَغْتَسِلُ فِي مَكَانٍ غَيْرِ طَيِّبٍ وَ مَا يُقَالُ عِنْدَ الْغُسْلِ وَ تَحْوِيلِ الْخَاتَمِ عِنْدَ الْغُسْلِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 29 – Description of the washing, and the Ablution (Wudhu) before it and after it, and the man washing in a place other than good, and what is to be said during the washing, and turning of the ring during the washing

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/134)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/134)
text: رَجُلٍ أَصَابَتْهُ جَنَابَةٌ فَقَامَ فِي الْمَطَرِ حَتَّى سَالَ عَلَى جَسَدِهِ أَ يُجْزِئُهُ ذَلِكَ مِنَ الْغُسْلِ قَالَ نَعَمْ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, and Abu Dawood, altogether from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Muhammad Bin Abu Hamza, from a man,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding a man who is hit by a sexual impurity, so he stands in the rain until it flows upon his body, ‘Would that suffice him from the washing?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes’.
en.sarwar: 7. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa and abu Dawud all from al-Husayn ibn Sa‘id from Muhammad ibn abu Hamzah from a man who has said the following: “I once asked abu ’Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who has had sexual relation after which he stands in the rain until water flows over his body. ‘Is this sufficient for Ghusl (bath)? He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, it is sufficient.’”
